events.event_label: RWC positively correlates with Fv/m and Fv/0
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p039.s001: Correlation analysis (Fig. 2) highlighted positive correlations recorded between RWC and the chlorophyll fluorescence traits Fv/m and Fv/0 (r = 0.91***, r = 0.64***, respectively). 10781440.p039.s002: Also, a positive correlation was observed between RWC and Chla (r = 0.69***) and Chlb (r = 0.86***), confirming the importance of RWC in the components of photosynthesis mechanisms under harsh environmental conditions. 10781440.p039.s003: Interestingly, RWC was shown to be negatively correlated with the osmo-regulators, which tend to increase under stress conditions, SSC and PC (r = −0.74*** and r = −0.46). 10781440.p039.s004: Moreover, Fv/m was positively correlated with Fv/0, Chla, and Chlb (r = 0.65***, r = 0.69***, r = 0.8***).

events.event_label: combined drought/heat stress increases osmolytes and secondary metabolites
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p059.s001: To survive combined drought/heat stress, plants change their metabolism to promote the synthesis of osmolytes and secondary metabolites that promote stress tolerance. 10781440.p059.s002: Plants affected by drought and heat stress accumulate compatible solutes, such as proline, glycine, betaine and soluble sugars, which play a role in osmotic adjustment to maintain water status and protect leaf cells. 10781440.p059.s003: Osmotic adjustment allows the plant to maintain turgor pressure at low water potential, which is important for maintaining metabolic functions. 10781440.p059.s004: The results showed that the genotypes Hedhba and Aouija showed the highest proline accumulation.

events.event_label: leaf proline accumulation correlates with yield
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p059.s006: Increasing the production of these osmoprotectants decreases ROS production and reduces leaf senescence. 10781440.p059.s007: Proline is part of many stress signalling pathways involved in stress adaptation. 10781440.p059.s008: A connection between leaf proline accumulation and yield stability under water and heat stresses has been reported in barley and wheat.

events.event_label: combined stress decreases growth and photosynthetic activity
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p061.s001: Our results show that the five durum wheat genotypes use the same strategies to tolerate combined stress, and emphasize the accumulation of single stresses that induced remarkable damage illustrated by the low growth and the sharp reduction in photosynthetic activity which will also influence production and yield later.

events.event_label: Fv/m positively correlates with Fv/0, Chla and Chlb
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p039.s003: Interestingly, RWC was shown to be negatively correlated with the osmo-regulators, which tend to increase under stress conditions, SSC and PC (r = −0.74*** and r = −0.46). 10781440.p039.s004: Moreover, Fv/m was positively correlated with Fv/0, Chla, and Chlb (r = 0.65***, r = 0.69***, r = 0.8***). 10781440.p039.s005: Positive associations were recorded between Chlb and Chla (r = 0.67***) and Fv/0 (r = 0.62***). 10781440.p039.s006: As expected, a positive correlation was recorded between SSC and PC (r = 0.58***). 10781440.p039.s007: Regarding the LT, this turned out to be positively correlated with PC and SSC (r = 0.5***, r = 0.7***, respectively).

events.event_label: severe combined stress decreases Quantum yield and photosynthetic efficiency
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p003.s006: The results showed that the combined stress significantly affected all the measured parameters. 10781440.p003.s007: The relative water content (RWC) decreased by 37.6% under T1 compared to T0. 10781440.p003.s008: Quantum yield (Fv/m) and photosynthetic efficiency (Fv/0) decreased under severe combined stress (T2) by 37.15% and 37.22%, respectively. 10781440.p003.s009: Under T2 stress, LT increased by 63.7%. 10781440.p003.s010: A significant increase in osmoprotective solutes was also observed, including proline, which increased by 154.6% under T2.

events.event_label: abiotic stresses reduce yield and quality
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p008.s001: Drought and heat stresses are the major factors limiting wheat productivity and yields. 10781440.p008.s002: Indeed, the two abiotic stresses cause reductions in the number and weight of grains, inducing significant losses in the yield and quality of cereals.

events.event_label: moderate and severe stress decrease photosynthetic efficiency (Fv/0)
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p029.s001: The results revealed that the photosynthetic efficiency (Fv/0) decreased under combined stress compared to the control. 10781440.p029.s002: Under moderate stress (T1), Fv/0 decreased by 28.02% compared to a decrease of 37.22% under severe stress (T2) compared to the control (T0) (Fig. 1B).

events.event_label: T1 and T2 decrease Chla
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p032.s001: Chlorophyll a and b content tends to decrease under combined stress. 10781440.p032.s002: Thus, under moderate stress (T1), the chlorophyll content decreased by 30.1%, compared to a decrease of 44.9% under severe stress (T2) (Fig. 1C). 10781440.p032.s003: Moreover, only the genotypes Karim and Hmira showed a significant decrease in Chla under both stress levels (T1 and T2) (Fig. 1D). 10781440.p032.s004: Interestingly, the Chla/Chlb ratio decreased for all the tested genotypes except for the genotype Karim.

events.event_label: moderate and severe stress increase LT
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p034.s001: Genotypic variability was observed for LT, with a general trend of increase under combined stress compared to control (T0) (Fig. 1F). 10781440.p034.s002: Under moderate stress (T1), LT increased by 16.97% compared to an increase of 26.03% under severe stress (T2) compared to control (T0) (Fig. 1F). 10781440.p034.s003: The highest mean LT was observed in the genotypes Hmira and Karim at 38.8 °C and 38.1 °C, respectively (Table 1).

events.event_label: combined stress increases soluble sugars (SSC)
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p036.s001: The applied stress resulted in an accumulation of soluble sugars (SSC) proportional to the severity of the combined stress. 10781440.p036.s002: The greatest increase in SSC was noted for Aouija under T2 with a 179% increase over the control (T0) (Fig. 1G).

events.event_label: stress increases PC
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p037.s001: The variation in PC was significantly (P < 0.001) affected by the different treatments applied (Table 1). 10781440.p037.s002: Thus, PC tends to increase under the effect of combined stress. 10781440.p037.s003: The maximum increase was recorded under severe stress (T2) with 154.6% and under moderate stress (T1) with 92.2%, compared to the control (T0). 10781440.p037.s004: The results showed that the genotype Hedhba has the highest PC (209.1%) under the two stress combinations, followed by Aouija with 199.6% (Fig. 1H).

events.event_label: Chlb positively correlates with Chla and Fv/0
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p039.s003: Interestingly, RWC was shown to be negatively correlated with the osmo-regulators, which tend to increase under stress conditions, SSC and PC (r = −0.74*** and r = −0.46). 10781440.p039.s004: Moreover, Fv/m was positively correlated with Fv/0, Chla, and Chlb (r = 0.65***, r = 0.69***, r = 0.8***). 10781440.p039.s005: Positive associations were recorded between Chlb and Chla (r = 0.67***) and Fv/0 (r = 0.62***). 10781440.p039.s006: As expected, a positive correlation was recorded between SSC and PC (r = 0.58***). 10781440.p039.s007: Regarding the LT, this turned out to be positively correlated with PC and SSC (r = 0.5***, r = 0.7***, respectively).

events.event_label: PC associates with SSC and Fv/0
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p044.s003: The second group is formed by the genotype Aouija. 10781440.p044.s004: Indeed, the ellipse of this group is in the opposite direction to that of the first group. 10781440.p044.s005: This group is characterized by PC, accompanied by quite significant SSC and Fv/0. 10781440.p044.s006: The third group is formed by the genotype Hedhba. 10781440.p044.s007: The second and third groups are narrowing in the same direction.

events.event_label: combined drought and heat stress decreases chlorophyll a and Chlb
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p054.s003: High temperatures destroy chlorophyll and damage plants by reducing light quantum acceptance. 10781440.p054.s004: Similarly, chlorophyll concentrations decreased more in plants exposed to combined drought and heat stress than individual stresses in wheat. 10781440.p054.s005: A decrease in chlorophyll a and b pigments under combined drought and heat stresses was reported in a stress-sensitive wheat cultivar. 10781440.p054.s006: Nonetheless, the calculation of the Cha/Chlb ratio under severe drought and heat stress revealed that the reduction of the two pigments was not in an equal way in all the genotypes. 10781440.p054.s007: Indeed, Biskri and Hmira had the highest decrease in Chla/Chlb under T2 treatment.

events.event_label: water correlates with RWC, chlorophyll b content, and Fv/0
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p003.s009: Under T2 stress, LT increased by 63.7%. 10781440.p003.s010: A significant increase in osmoprotective solutes was also observed, including proline, which increased by 154.6% under T2. 10781440.p003.s011: Correlation analyses of the combination of water and heat stress confirm that the traits RWC, chlorophyll b content, Fv/m, proline content, Fv/0 and leaf temperature can be used as reliable screening criteria for the two stresses combined. 10781440.p003.s012: The principal component analysis highlighted that Aouija tolerates the two levels of stresses studied, while the genotypes Karim and Hmira are the most sensitive. 10781440.p003.s013: The results show that the tolerance of durum wheat to combined water and heat stress involves several adaptation mechanisms proportional to the stress intensity.

events.event_label: combined stress increases PC (higher under severe stress)

events.event_label: moderate and severe stress decrease chlorophyll content
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p032.s001: Chlorophyll a and b content tends to decrease under combined stress. 10781440.p032.s002: Thus, under moderate stress (T1), the chlorophyll content decreased by 30.1%, compared to a decrease of 44.9% under severe stress (T2) (Fig. 1C). 10781440.p032.s003: Moreover, only the genotypes Karim and Hmira showed a significant decrease in Chla under both stress levels (T1 and T2) (Fig. 1D). 10781440.p032.s004: Interestingly, the Chla/Chlb ratio decreased for all the tested genotypes except for the genotype Karim.

events.event_label: water deficit and high temperature decrease Fv/m and affect PSII
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p058.s001: A reduction in Fv/m by water deficit and high temperature suggests a possible inhibition of PSII photochemistry, which may be due to an inadequate relocation of the energy of the light-harvesting chlorophyll complex towards the reaction centre. 10781440.p058.s002: A minimal decrease in Fv/m was observed for Aouija indicating that higher defensive capacity for PSII is a key resistance mechanism for durum wheat landraces, which is in agreement with previous studies for durum wheat accessions under water and heat stress conditions. 10781440.p058.s003: For the parameters reported in this work, Aouija is more tolerant to abiotic stress and could be an opportunity for breeding programs. 10781440.p058.s004: It is important to remember that these morpho-physiological parameters were defined by low rates of decrease in the genotypes Aouija and Hedhba, which suggests that the latter two are tolerant to combined stress despite the accumulation of stress effects.

events.event_label: drought and heat stress induce leaf senescence reducing photosynthesis and yield
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p054.s001: In addition, drought and heat stress can induce leaf senescence, leading to chlorophyll degradation and photosynthesis disruption, which hamper crop yield. 10781440.p054.s002: Chlorophyll is the main pigment for photosynthesis and is most sensitive to high temperatures and water stress. 10781440.p054.s003: High temperatures destroy chlorophyll and damage plants by reducing light quantum acceptance. 10781440.p054.s004: Similarly, chlorophyll concentrations decreased more in plants exposed to combined drought and heat stress than individual stresses in wheat.

events.event_label: T1 and T2 treatments decrease chlorophyll a, chlorophyll b, FV/0, Fv/m, RWC and increase leaf area and PC
Water and heat stress increase sugars and SSC
events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p060.s001: Under conditions of water and heat stress, plants accumulate other osmotically active substances like sugars. 10781440.p060.s002: Our data showed a substantial increase in this solute correlated with the severity of applied stress, as previously reported. 10781440.p060.s003: The increase in SSC was higher under the treatments at (T2) than under (T1) and this trend was particularly remarkable for Aouija than for the other genotypes. 10781440.p060.s004: Sensitive plants showed less increase in SSC than tolerant plants.

events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p058.s001: A reduction in Fv/m by water deficit and high temperature suggests a possible inhibition of PSII photochemistry, which may be due to an inadequate relocation of the energy of the light-harvesting chlorophyll complex towards the reaction centre. 10781440.p058.s002: A minimal decrease in Fv/m was observed for Aouija indicating that higher defensive capacity for PSII is a key resistance mechanism for durum wheat landraces, which is in agreement with previous studies for durum wheat accessions under water and heat stress conditions. 10781440.p058.s003: For the parameters reported in this work, Aouija is more tolerant to abiotic stress and could be an opportunity for breeding programs. 10781440.p058.s004: It is important to remember that these morpho-physiological parameters were defined by low rates of decrease in the genotypes Aouija and Hedhba, which suggests that the latter two are tolerant to combined stress despite the accumulation of stress effects.

events.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p056.s001: In addition, chlorophyll fluorescence analysis revealed that applied stress results in a significant decrease in Fv/m for all studied genotypes. 10781440.p056.s002: This decrease can be explained by a reduction in the transfer of electrons to the reaction centre of the PSII due to variations in energy absorption, electron trapping, transport and dissipation per section, leading to a reduction in photosynthetic efficiency of PSII. 10781440.p056.s003: According to, water stress followed by heat stress progressively decreases electron transport from PSII and increases non-photochemical quenching in wheat leaves that support the role of alternative sink electrons (from either PSII or PSI) and the flow of cyclic electrons for the photoprotection of PSII and PSI, which also generate the ATP needed to cope with combined stress conditions. 10781440.p056.s004: In this sense, the chlorophyll fluorescence of the genotype Aouija was the least affected.

evidence.text: According to, water stress followed by heat stress progressively decreases electron transport from PSII and increases non-photochemical quenching in wheat leaves that support the role of alternative sink electrons (from either PSII or PSI) and the flow of cyclic electrons for the photoprotection of PSII and PSI, which also generate the ATP needed to cope with combined stress conditions.

relations.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p056.s001: In addition, chlorophyll fluorescence analysis revealed that applied stress results in a significant decrease in Fv/m for all studied genotypes. 10781440.p056.s002: This decrease can be explained by a reduction in the transfer of electrons to the reaction centre of the PSII due to variations in energy absorption, electron trapping, transport and dissipation per section, leading to a reduction in photosynthetic efficiency of PSII. 10781440.p056.s003: According to, water stress followed by heat stress progressively decreases electron transport from PSII and increases non-photochemical quenching in wheat leaves that support the role of alternative sink electrons (from either PSII or PSI) and the flow of cyclic electrons for the photoprotection of PSII and PSI, which also generate the ATP needed to cope with combined stress conditions. 10781440.p056.s004: In this sense, the chlorophyll fluorescence of the genotype Aouija was the least affected.

relations.subject: water deficit

evidence.text: A reduction in Fv/m by water deficit and high temperature suggests a possible inhibition of PSII photochemistry, which may be due to an inadequate relocation of the energy of the light-harvesting chlorophyll complex towards the reaction centre.

relations.evidence_preview: A reduction in Fv/m by water deficit and high temperature suggests a possible inhibition of PSII photochemistry, which may be due to an inadequate relocation of the energy of the light-harvesting chlorophyll complex towards the reaction centre.
relations.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p058.s001: A reduction in Fv/m by water deficit and high temperature suggests a possible inhibition of PSII photochemistry, which may be due to an inadequate relocation of the energy of the light-harvesting chlorophyll complex towards the reaction centre. 10781440.p058.s002: A minimal decrease in Fv/m was observed for Aouija indicating that higher defensive capacity for PSII is a key resistance mechanism for durum wheat landraces, which is in agreement with previous studies for durum wheat accessions under water and heat stress conditions. 10781440.p058.s003: For the parameters reported in this work, Aouija is more tolerant to abiotic stress and could be an opportunity for breeding programs. 10781440.p058.s004: It is important to remember that these morpho-physiological parameters were defined by low rates of decrease in the genotypes Aouija and Hedhba, which suggests that the latter two are tolerant to combined stress despite the accumulation of stress effects.

relations.subject: combined drought/heat stress

evidence.text: To survive combined drought/heat stress, plants change their metabolism to promote the synthesis of osmolytes and secondary metabolites that promote stress tolerance.

relations.evidence_preview: To survive combined drought/heat stress, plants change their metabolism to promote the synthesis of osmolytes and secondary metabolites that promote stress tolerance.
relations.evidence_context_text: 10781440.p059.s001: To survive combined drought/heat stress, plants change their metabolism to promote the synthesis of osmolytes and secondary metabolites that promote stress tolerance. 10781440.p059.s002: Plants affected by drought and heat stress accumulate compatible solutes, such as proline, glycine, betaine and soluble sugars, which play a role in osmotic adjustment to maintain water status and protect leaf cells. 10781440.p059.s003: Osmotic adjustment allows the plant to maintain turgor pressure at low water potential, which is important for maintaining metabolic functions. 10781440.p059.s004: The results showed that the genotypes Hedhba and Aouija showed the highest proline accumulation.

To survive combined drought/heat stress, plants change their metabolism to promote the synthesis of osmolytes and secondary metabolites that promote stress tolerance.

Plants affected by drought and heat stress accumulate compatible solutes, such as proline, glycine, betaine and soluble sugars, which play a role in osmotic adjustment to maintain water status and protect leaf cells.
Increasing the production of these osmoprotectants decreases ROS production and reduces leaf senescence.
Proline is part of many stress signalling pathways involved in stress adaptation.
A connection between leaf proline accumulation and yield stability under water and heat stresses has been reported in barley and wheat.

Sensitive plants showed less increase in SSC than tolerant plants.

Our results show that the five durum wheat genotypes use the same strategies to tolerate combined stress, and emphasize the accumulation of single stresses that induced remarkable damage illustrated by the low growth and the sharp reduction in photosynthetic activity which will also influence production and yield later.
